# WI-QUIC-CRT-0021 - QUIC CRT client trust-material and peer-identity validation work item

## Summary

Add the smallest honest managed-client validation seam so explicit trust material and an exact peer identity can be carried through the current client path and fail closed on mismatch without widening the public API or trust-store behavior.

## Requirements Addressed

- REQ-QUIC-CRT-0123

## Design Inputs

- ARC-QUIC-CRT-0021

## Planned Changes

- Introduce an internal immutable client certificate-policy snapshot that carries exact peer identity and explicit trust material.
- Thread a narrow internal peer-identity carrier through the current managed client settings capture and runtime bridge path.
- Preserve the existing public client surface and reject-first option validation while adding the smallest honest trust-material decision point.
- Make the client acceptance decision fail closed when the trust material does not match or the exact peer identity does not match.
- Add positive and negative requirement-home tests for matching trust and identity, trust mismatch, identity mismatch, missing trust material, and missing identity.
- Keep OS trust-store integration, generalized certificate-path building, revocation, broader client-auth, 0-RTT, key update, transfer, and retry out of scope.

## Out of Scope

- OS trust-store integration
- revocation
- generalized certificate-path building
- broader client-auth
- 0-RTT
- key update
- public API widening
- transfer enablement
- retry enablement

## Verification Plan

Run the new client-validation requirement-home tests, the current CRT and API guards that cover the managed client path, the full `REQ_QUIC_CRT_` sweep, and the repo-local SpecTrace validation/render checks after the canonical JSON changes.

## Completion Notes

Implemented the managed-client exact-trust and exact-peer-identity floor and closed the requirement-home coverage under REQ-QUIC-CRT-0123; the broader standard BCL-shaped validation path is traced separately under REQ-QUIC-API-0013.
